name: FluidType
description: Valve fluid type

name: ModeCheck
description: Global check for mode change warnings while still in Process mode

name: FlowType
description: Valve compressible, incompressible, or flow range calculations

name: CheckValve
description: Valve check valve to prevent reverse flow

name: Tc
description: Valve time to open/close

name: xT
description: Valve critical flow pressure ratio coefficient

name: CpCv
description: Valve gas heat capacity ratio

name: FRramp
description:
Global flow parameter to ramp from forward stream
conditions to reverse stream conditions.

For steady state simulations, use a value smaller than
the smallest flowrate in your simulation.

For dynamic simulations, the FRramp will cause the
thermodynamic state calculations to use the forward
enthalpy/composition when the flow is greater than
FRramp, reverse enthalpy/composition when flow is
less than negative FRramp, and ramp the values in
between negative FRramp and FRramp as flow reversal
takes place.

A larger value will smooth out sudden discontinuities
for dynamics but stream properties may appear wrong
near zero flow in steady state simulations.

name: Elevations
description:
Global selection to enable the specification of equipment port elevations.
     Disabled: disable specifications for port elevations.  The elevation change (DE) can still be specified in the Pipe and PipeRig models.
     Manual: manually specify port elevations. DE in the Pipe and PipeRig models can be swapped for port elevation specifications.
     Automatic: automatically calculate port elevations using equipment geometry and configuration options for select models (Column, Drum, etc).

When automatic elevations are enabled, an elevation specification must be removed from each inlet stream of impacted models (for example, remove the DE specification from an inlet Pipe).
Other model elevations must still be manually specified. For an explanation of how port elevations are estimated, see each model's help file.

name: Char
description: Valve characteristic curve

name: ActuatorType
description: Valve actuator type: first order or constant velocity

name: Leak
description: Valve leak fraction

name: MotiveModelType
description:
Valve motive power replaceable submodel.
     Keep 'NoMotivePwr' submodel if the valve does not have a defined motive power.
     Select 'MotiveAir' submodel to set air as the motive power.
     Create a copy of 'MotiveAir' and rename variable 'Air' to customize motive power/fluid for the valve.

name: FailPosition
description:
Valve fail position selection.
     Closed: valve closes when motive power failed or tripped.
     Open: valve opens when motive power failed or tripped.
     Last: valve holds the last known position when motive power failed or tripped.

name: Phases
description:
Valve option to override the fluid phase selection
     Fluid: The FluidType's phases are applied.
     Vapor: The phases are set to Vapor.
     Liquid: The phases are set to Liquid.
     VLE: The phases are set to VLE.
     VLWE: The phases are set to VLWE.
     VLLE: The phases are set to VLLE.

name: PhaseNoOverride
description: Valve PhaseNoOverride parameter defaulted to NoOverride which is used for the connection to the State.PhaseOverride when Phases = Fluid

name: RFState
description:
Global option to calculate valve flow from forward and reverse stream properties.
     Single -  Forward and reverse stream properties are mixed in a single flash to
                   calculate the properties in the direction of flow.
     Double - Forward and reverse stream properties are flashed separately
                   and only the properties in the flow direction are used.
The RFState option is applicable only when FlowType is either Compressible or Incompressible.

name: DPlin
description: Valve pressure difference at which flow is assumed to be linear with pressure drop.\nAdjusting value may improve convergence at low flow and reverse flow conditions.

name: LineSizedCv
description:
Valve line sized Cv calculation:
     Actual: specify Cv or calculate from DP, flowrate and position.
     Other Cv options: calculate Cv based on line size and valve type.
          See Submodels.ValveCoeff help (Crane Technical Paper 410)
          for details. Go to PipeData group to select pipe.

name: Standard
description:
Global Standard options:
     None: ignore piping standards and allow the pipe inside diameter 
          to be calculated/specified to any value.
     NPS: specify the pipe diameter in Nominal Pipe Size.
     DN: specify the pipe diameter in Diameter Nominal.
     JIS A: specify the pipe diameter in Japanese Industrial Standard A.
     JIS B: specify the pipe diameter in Japanese Industrial Standard B.
     GOST: specify the pipe diameter in Russian Standard.
     DIN: specify the pipe diameter in German Standard.

name: DefaultPipeSize
description: Valve default pipe size.

name: PipeSizing
description: Valve PipeSizing parameter set to 'Fixed' to use the fixed DefaultPipeSize.
